sources.note: 24 hybrid/EV listings read; richest source (VINs + photos).
Reachable, no captcha; cheapest-first sub-$10k results were all gas beaters. No hybrid/EV surfaced without deeper fuel filtering.
Searched prius/leaf/volt/hybrid queries; many private-seller results.
Logged in; searched prius + nissan leaf, dozens of tiles. Photos are signed/ephemeral so left null.
Hybrid inventory priced ~$25k+; nothing at or under $10k.
BIN results are nationwide with unverified distance-from-80015; none beat local deals, so none shortlisted.
Searched prius near Denver/Colo. Springs (defaulted 80mi); clean year/price/mileage tiles.
Search URL returns HTTP 404; site layout changed and typically has no sub-$10k inventory.
Search URL 404s; site price tiers start at 'Under $20k' — no sub-$10k hybrids/EVs exist.
Utah-only; well beyond the 100-mi radius of 80015. Not stretching the radius this run.

listings.carSeatNotes: Midsize sedan with a roomy rear bench and easy LATCH access — rear-facing seat fits comfortably.
listings.nextAction: Dealer — request the free Carfax link and proof the 9 model-year recalls were performed.
listings.why: Lowest-mileage car on the list (101k) and a genuinely roomy, safe family sedan. 4-star NHTSA, ~38-41 mpg. Best all-round pick for a young child.

listings.carSeatNotes: Tall wagon body with wide-opening doors — very easy to load a rear-facing seat and daycare bags in the hatch.
listings.nextAction: Ask this private seller for the VIN + title photo and current mileage before driving out.
listings.why: Cheapest plug-in and right here in Aurora. ~19 mi electric covers most of the daily commute on the Level-1 outlet, then 38 mpg hybrid. Mileage not posted — verify.

listings.carSeatNotes: Full midsize sedan — the largest rear seat here; rear-facing installs with room to spare and easy LATCH.
listings.nextAction: Dealer — request Carfax and confirm the 9 recall campaigns were completed.
listings.why: 5-star NHTSA, roomy well-equipped Limited trim, modern safety kit for a child. Newest safety features of the sedans and close to home.

listings.carSeatNotes: Roomy midsize sedan rear seat, easy LATCH; note the plug-in battery slightly reduces trunk depth for a stroller.
listings.nextAction: Ask the private seller for VIN, title status and mileage; confirm the 13 model-year recalls were addressed.
listings.why: NEW. 5-star midsize plug-in sedan under $7k. ~20 mi EV covers much of the commute on Level 1, then efficient hybrid. Greeley is ~54 mi — factor the drive to inspect.

listings.carSeatNotes: Roomy hatchback rear seat fits a rear-facing seat; tall roofline makes lowering a child in easy, big hatch for gear.
listings.nextAction: Dealer — request Carfax; verify the 10 model-year recalls (incl. any inverter campaigns) were completed.
listings.why: Bulletproof reliability (200k+ routine), 50 mpg, roomy hatch, only 12 mi away. High miles but very cheap to run.

listings.carSeatNotes: 2nd-gen Volt gained rear-seat room; rear-facing fits but the sloping roof means checking headroom on install.
listings.nextAction: Ask the private seller for VIN, mileage and title photo before the ~54 mi drive to Greeley.
listings.why: 2nd-gen Volt: ~53 mi electric range covers the full ~25 mi commute on the home Level-1 outlet, gas backup = zero range anxiety. 5-star, 0 open recalls. Mileage not listed — confirm.

listings.carSeatNotes: Roomy Prius liftback rear seat; easy rear-facing fit and a big hatch for stroller + daycare bags.
listings.nextAction: Private party — verify clean title and get maintenance records; confirm recent inspection claim.
listings.why: Newer-gen Prius, top MPG of the group, seller notes a recent inspection, only 12 mi away. Only 3 model-year recalls.

listings.carSeatNotes: 1st-gen Volt has a firm center console and only two rear seats — check rear-facing clearance behind the front passenger.
listings.nextAction: Dealer — request Carfax and the 1 recall receipt; ask about traction-battery health.
listings.why: Cheapest plug-in with full data: ~35 mi electric daily commute on the home outlet, gas backup for trips. 5-star NHTSA, only 1 recall.

listings.carSeatNotes: Hatchback with a flat, roomy rear bench — rear-facing seat installs easily; good door opening for a child.
listings.nextAction: Ask the private seller for a photo of the dashboard battery-capacity bars (or a LeafSpy report) and mileage before viewing.
listings.why: NEW. Cheap, close-by 30 kWh EV — easily covers the short commute on Level 1. Winter range drops ~25-30%, so confirm battery bars. Only 2 recalls.

listings.carSeatNotes: Subcompact — rear-facing fits but you may need to slide the front passenger seat forward; verify clearance with the seat installed.
listings.nextAction: Private seller in Aurora — ask for VIN + title photo and confirm the ~100k mileage; easy local test-drive.
listings.why: NEW and local to Aurora. Newer, very reliable, 46 mpg, low-ish miles (100k), 0 open recalls. Small back seat is the only caveat for a rear-facing seat.

listings.carSeatNotes: Roomy hatchback rear seat with easy LATCH; flat floor makes rear-facing installs simple.
listings.nextAction: Dealer — request Carfax and a battery state-of-health readout; confirm the 4 recalls were addressed.
listings.why: 40 kWh pack = much better real-world/winter range than older Leafs; top SL trim. Higher miles and 55 mi away in Colorado Springs.

listings.carSeatNotes: Compact SUV with an upright rear seat and tall door opening — easy, no-bending rear-facing installs and lots of cargo for kid gear.
listings.nextAction: Dealer — request Carfax; on a 2007 hybrid, ask specifically about traction-battery replacement history.
listings.why: Cheapest legit hybrid and an SUV, so the most kid-gear space. Reliable ex-fleet platform. Lower MPG, older, and 54 mi out in Greeley. Cars.com 'Great Deal'.
